danger_signals.visual_form: Color shift from green to red in bottom-left corner stamina display
danger_signals.meaning: Player fatigue from combat; continued depletion results in ejection from bike

danger_signals.visual_form: Bike condition meter displayed between racer and opponent stamina meters; decreases with crashes
danger_signals.meaning: Cumulative vehicle damage; full depletion wrecks bike and ends race participation

danger_signals.visual_form: Sporadic appearance of police officers on tracks
danger_signals.meaning: Hazard that can apprehend racer post-crash, resulting in fines and potential game-over if funds insufficient

danger_signals.visual_form: Cash balance deduction for repair bills and fines
danger_signals.meaning: Inability to cover repair or fine costs triggers premature game-over

danger_signals.visual_form: Boulders, cars, deer, and cows visible on track
danger_signals.meaning: Collision hazards that eject player from bike and trigger stamina/condition penalties

danger_signals.visual_form: Visual representation of clubs and chains held by enemy racers
danger_signals.meaning: Immediate threat of armed combat; weapons can be seized if attacker is struck first

safety_signals.visual_form: Green-colored stamina meter in bottom-left corner at full or high capacity
safety_signals.meaning: Player capable of sustained combat; low injury risk from further engagement

safety_signals.visual_form: Condition meter showing high status; inverse of crash accumulation
safety_signals.meaning: Vehicle stable for continued racing without immediate wreck risk

safety_signals.visual_form: Accumulated cash balance above minimum required for repair bills/fines
safety_signals.meaning: Sufficient economic buffer to continue game despite penalties

safety_signals.visual_form: Racer position at front of visible race field
safety_signals.meaning: Reduced direct combat pressure; fewer opponents ahead to compete with

safety_signals.visual_form: Track segments without visible police officer presence
safety_signals.meaning: Temporary reprieve from apprehension hazard; safe passage possible

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Player motorcycle visually distinct from 14 AI-controlled opponent racers; player stamina displayed bottom-left
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Player position highlighted in split-screen multiplayer mode; controllable character with forward-facing perspective

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Distinct motorcycle models and racer appearances; vary in aggression with progression through difficulty levels; some wield weapons
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Opponent stamina visible bottom-right corner; increase in number and aggression across five levels (Hawaii → Arizona → Tennessee → Alaska → Vermont)

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Police officer visual identity implied through context
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Distinct from racers; static obstacles rather than competing entities; sporadic placement
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Appear more frequently and extract higher fines as game progresses through difficulty levels; represent state authority outside racing competition

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Tropical environment; lower police frequency; smaller cash prizes visible in UI
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: AI opponents less aggressive; repair bills and fines lower; racer can recover from mistakes more easily

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Desert and rural environments; increased police presence; longer track layouts
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Opponent aggression scales; repair costs and fines increase; combat encounters more frequent and dangerous

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Arctic and mountainous environments; high police density; extended course complexity
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Maximum opponent aggression; substantial financial penalties; cumulative difficulty creates high-stakes racing tension

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Opponent stamina meter visible bottom-right; weapons displayed in opponent hands; both racer and opponent stamina shift toward red
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Both entities engage in punch/kick/weapon attacks; stamina depletion risk increases; tactical combat positioning required

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Racer ejected from bike; on-foot running animation begins; stamina meter reset or depleted entirely
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player loses combat capability; automatic return-to-bike behavior; vulnerability to incoming traffic and police

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Police officer encounters ejected racer; fine deduction from cash display
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Race participation ended; financial penalty applied; potential game-over if funds insufficient

environmental_storytelling.example: Game traverses five distinct US regions (Hawaii → Arizona → Tennessee → Alaska → Vermont), with each location visually distinct but thematically reinforcing increasing hazard severity and isolation. Arctic environments imply harsher conditions than tropical starting point.

Boulders, cars, deer, and cows serve as regional environmental storytelling—suggesting authentic local hazards (wildlife in rural areas, traffic in populated zones) without explicit narrative exposition.
Increasing police presence across levels suggests escalating state enforcement or transition from lawless to lawful jurisdictions, visually communicating world-state shift without dialogue.

Repair bills and fines grow more expensive with level progression, implying regions with stricter law enforcement, more expensive services, or higher economic stakes—conveyed entirely through numeric and visual cost displays.

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Can engage in sustained combat; offensive capability maintained; can pursue or flee opponents effectively
Combat weakened; ejection imminent; defensive positioning required; risk of forced dismount
Automatic return-to-bike behavior; can alter course with directional input; vulnerable to traffic and police; reduced agency until remounted
Offensive threat increased; weapon can be seized if opponent is struck while holding it; indicates aggression
Less aggressive engagement; lower stamina depletion rate; beatable with basic tactics
Highly aggressive; frequent weapon use; tactical combat positioning; requires sustained engagement
Apprehends ejected racer; applies financial penalty; ends race participation if player cannot escape
Sporadic appearances; low penalty threat; manageable obstacle
Frequent appearances; high penalty threat; significant game-over risk if cash insufficient

notes: Road Rash II communicates world-state primarily through UI visualization (color-coded stamina meters), environmental variation (geographic progression), and emergent difficulty scaling (opponent aggression, police frequency, financial penalties). No explicit faction conflict or political narrative exists; instead, the game presents a graduated progression from accessible to punishing. The split-screen multiplayer mode reinforces competitive player-vs-player dynamics while maintaining identical visual communication systems. World-state shifts are communicated implicitly through cost escalation and environmental hazard density rather than explicit exposition.
